mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines across a 5-year walk-forward test, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.5pp monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of final goods and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand recovery, driving sustained corporate revenue growth and positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both grow > 2% MoM
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side bottlenecks and rising input costs, compressing profit margins and generating negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions during tightening cycles produce positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in core inflation below 2.0% removes upward pressure on discount rates, allowing equity valuations to expand and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings below 2.0% are followed by negative 5-day returns in > 50% of observations over a 4-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls indicate weakening economic activity and reduced consumer spending capacity, leading to lower corporate earnings expectations and negative quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines show no negative correlation with 60-day forward returns, or the signal Rank IC remains below -0.02 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where real sales of final goods grow faster than real GDP signals improving corporate profitability and margin expansion, driving positive monthly S&P 500 returns as earnings revisions accelerate.
failure_condition: The RSAFS-GDPC1 growth spread fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 52% over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in industrial production below 98.0 during a recessionary phase signals severe supply chain disruption and falling output, causing immediate risk aversion and negative weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO readings below 98.0 during recessions are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the trigger condition never activates in historical data.
